entry.title: How do you identify and segregate the list of Pay components which will be maintained in EC or ECP?
entry.content:
The "Payroll Process Strategy Architecture Leading Practice" document identifies the need to "Identify and segregate the list of Pay components which will be maintained in Employee Central or Employee Central payroll." (page 30)

Our approach has been to use EC for all pay components (including employee deductions) but are there any conditions or approaches for when pay components/wagetypes are maintained directly in ECP (through the MashUp)? I've tried to find an IDP but there doesn't seem to be a guiding principle.

Appreciate any feedback

entry.title: sales order approval notification is Not available in user launchpad
entry.content:
Dear Sap,

We have noticed that whenever there is a credit limit for approval sent from a sales order, there is no notification showing on our Launchpad. But for purchase order and outgoing payment approval there is.

Pls check what is missing on our steps in order to display such notification.

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: sales order approval notification is Not available in...
entry.content: The approval tasks for CRM documents are not available in inbox, hence they are not found in the launchpad. You may find more details in KBA:  2993901 - Approval Task for CRM Documents Are Not Visible in Inbox, for Example "Approve Sales Order" Task

entry.title: Re: Getting the contents of a command
entry.content:
Yes, you can. You need to use the RAS object model (ReportClientDocument and SubreportClientDocument). Target Tables of type "CommandTable". 

The image below shows my report inspector listing the SQL of Commands in a main report as well as a subreport. Unlike the SQL for regular tables, the SQL for Commands can be extracted without connecting to the data source:

entry.title: Teams and Responsibilities integration with SucessFactors Org Structure
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to ask the community if anyone had done an integration between Teams and Responsibilities integration with SucessFactors Org Structure?

If yes, please provide steps / suggestions on how to proceed with this one.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

I category myself as a women and first generation college student.

I am excited to learn SAP as I was not exposed to this during my study year. I join several career talk where they said SAP is a necessary skill to boost yourself during job hunting as many large company are using it. I look forward to participating in this initiative.

entry.title: Re: Planned Orders list of the last MRP run
entry.content:
Hello @Suresh_Eswaran 

Thanks for your question. Certainly! In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, managing and filtering planned orders after an MRP run can be crucial for efficient planning and execution. Here are a few approaches to meet your requirement:

1. Using the "Manage Planned Orders" App with Filters: The "Manage Planned Orders" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud allows you to filter and sort planned orders based on various criteria. Although it shows all planned orders, you can use the following steps to filter out the newly created planned orders:Open the "Manage Planned Orders" App - Navigate to the app from your Fiori Launchpad.Use the filter options to set the creation date to today’s date. This will help you filter out the planned orders created by the last MRP run.
- You can also filter by priority if you have a custom field or a way to identify priority planned orders. Then Save this filter as a variant for quick access in the future.

2.Using Custom CDS Views: If the standard apps do not meet your requirements, you can create a custom CDS (Core Data Services) view to fetch the planned orders created by the last MRP run. Use the Custom CDS Views app to create a new view (Include the necessary fields such as planned order number, creation date, and priority) - Add a filter condition to only include planned orders created on the current date. Then  Expose this CDS view as an OData service or use it in a custom analytical query.

3. Using Analytical Queries:  You can also create an analytical query to fetch the planned orders created by the last MRP run.Use the "Custom Analytical Queries" app to create a new query. Select the data source related to planned orders(Add a filter for the creation date to today’s date) Optionally, add filters for priority if needed. Save the query and execute it to get the list of planned orders created by the last MRP run.

By using these three approaches, you can effectively filter and manage planned orders created by the last MRP run, ensuring that planners can focus on priority orders.

Hope it can help.

Best Regards

Dave
